content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

Two of three amendments identify real, verified bugs. Amendment fr-19-731dc375 is a concrete prompt-integrity issue where unvalidated mapped_to_bead_id biases voting panels with false 'no restart' promises. Amendment fr-19-ff125949 closes a defense-in-depth gap in reconstruction. Amendment fr-19-434f4f1d describes a scenario that doesn't hold under code inspection — reconstruction reads from the aggregate (which exists), not the failed journal, so the dedup won't block the re-insertion.

## Votes

- `fr-19-ff125949`: Accept
  Verified in code: reconstruct_missing_pe_mappings (line 975) iterates final_accepted_amendments from deserialized aggregate JSON and uses mapped_to_bead_id directly without an extract_pe_bead_ids allowlist check. Both engine.rs (line 7641) and final_review.rs (line 1516) perform this validation, establishing a clear convention. The reconstruction path reads from disk files that could be corrupted, from older code versions, or tampered with. Adding the allowlist check here is a targeted, low-risk change that closes the defense-in-depth gap and maintains the fail-closed invariant across all PE mapping paths.
- `fr-19-731dc375`: Accept
  Verified in code: build_voter_prompt is called at lines 644 and 879 with raw amendments before any allowlist validation. Lines 1897-1906 inject an explicit 'will NOT trigger a restart; the concern will be recorded as already covered by the mapped-to bead' note for any amendment with a non-empty mapped_to_bead_id. The extract_pe_bead_ids validation at line 1516 only runs AFTER all voting completes. A hallucinated or out-of-scope bead ID in mapped_to_bead_id will (1) promise voters the amendment is free/no-restart, (2) bias them toward acceptance, (3) then get stripped post-vote by the validation, converting it into a restart-triggering amendment. This is a semantic integrity bug with real consequences — the fix (validate and strip invalid mapped_to_bead_id before building prompts) is straightforward and important.
- `fr-19-434f4f1d`: Reject
  The described failure scenario does not hold under code inspection. Line 965 shows rounds_to_scan only contains the authoritative_max_round. If round N+1's aggregate exists on disk (which it must for the round-filtering at lines 744-753 to consider round N+1 authoritative), then reconstruction will process round N+1's aggregate. The seen_keys set at lines 942-951 is built from existing_mappings (journal entries). If round N+1's journal write failed, that mapping won't be in seen_keys, so reconstruction from round N+1's aggregate will succeed and re-insert it. The dedup only blocks if the same identity key was already successfully persisted in the journal — exactly the case where reconstruction is unnecessary. The planner's rejection analysis is correct.
